source.reference: Standard VLE flash calculation - benzene/toluene binary system at 1 atm. Commonly found in Smith/Van Ness/Abbott 'Introduction to Chemical Engineering Thermodynamics' and Seader/Henley 'Separation Process Principles'. Values verified via Raoult's law with Antoine equation.

problem.statement: An equimolar feed of benzene and toluene (50 mol% each) at 100 kmol/h is fed to a flash drum operating at 1 atm (101.325 kPa). The feed is 25% vaporized (vapor fraction = 0.25). Determine the compositions of the liquid and vapor leaving the separator, and estimate the drum temperature.
problem.task: Calculate: (1) mole fraction of benzene in the liquid product, (2) mole fraction of benzene in the vapor product, (3) flash drum temperature, (4) liquid and vapor flow rates.

feed_flow_rate.description: Total molar flow rate of feed

feed_composition_benzene.description: Mole fraction of benzene in feed

feed_composition_toluene.description: Mole fraction of toluene in feed

vapor_fraction.description: Fraction of feed that is vaporized (V/F)

liquid_benzene_mole_fraction.description: Mole fraction of benzene in liquid product (x_benzene)

vapor_benzene_mole_fraction.description: Mole fraction of benzene in vapor product (y_benzene)

flash_temperature.description: Temperature in the flash drum (approximate, depends on Antoine constants used)

liquid_flow_rate.description: Liquid product flow rate = F * (1 - V/F)

flash_temperature._comment: Within 3 K — varies with Antoine constant source

liquid_flow_rate._comment: Within 1% — this is just arithmetic from V/F

must_include: Identify this as a VLE flash calculation
Use Raoult's law or appropriate VLE correlation
State or use Antoine equation for vapor pressures
must_not_include: Assume ideal gas behavior without stating the assumption
Confuse mole fraction with mass fraction

domain_context.recommended_thermo_model: Raoult's law (appropriate for benzene/toluene at 1 atm — nearly ideal system)
key_assumptions: Ideal liquid mixture (benzene and toluene are chemically similar)
Thermal equilibrium between liquid and vapor
common_mistakes: Using wrong Antoine constant units (check if ln vs log10, Pa vs mmHg)
Forgetting that V/F is given, not temperature — this is a T-unknown flash
Applying Raoult's law without checking if the system is approximately ideal
Mixing up x (liquid) and y (vapor) compositions
Not closing the material balance — compositions should satisfy z = x(1-V/F) + y(V/F)

reasoning_checkpoints.checkpoint: Correctly identifies problem type as isothermal flash with specified V/F
Selects Raoult's law as appropriate VLE model for this system
Looks up or calculates Antoine equation parameters for benzene and toluene
Sets up the Rachford-Rice equation or equivalent flash equation
Solves iteratively for temperature (since T is the unknown)
Verifies answer by checking material balance closure
